article_title: Tesla Debuts Third-Generation Optimus at Shanghai Expo, Targets Mass Production by Year-End
summary: Round 2 re-review: APPROVE. The contributor addressed all five issues from the initial REQUEST_CHANGES review. Fortune misattributions removed, TechCrunch misattribution corrected, unsourced technical specs replaced with softer claims, three new sources (CNBC, Fox Business, TechCrunch Feb 2026) added and verified. All claims now trace to cited sources.

editor_notes.source_verification: Round 2 re-review. All 5 sources verified: (1) Rest of World — confirmed China >90% humanoid robot sales, Lian Jye Su/Omdia analyst quote, Chinese manufacturing advantages. (2) Fortune — confirmed 'agonizingly slow' quote, S-curve ramp, 'almost everything is new', low/high production timeline. (3) CNBC — confirmed Model S/X production ending, Fremont conversion to Optimus, '1 million unit per year' capacity target (direct Musk quote). (4) Fox Business — confirmed Davos/WEF appearance, 2027 public sales timeline, reliability/safety quote verbatim. (5) TechCrunch (Feb 2026) — confirmed China outpacing U.S. in humanoid robots, faster iteration.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims now trace to cited sources. AWE 2026 Shanghai (March 12) is referenced without attribution but is a widely reported public event — acceptable. Previously flagged Fortune misattributions, TechCrunch misattribution, and unsourced technical specs have all been resolved.
editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with balanced What We Know / What We Don't Know sections. Proper skepticism of Tesla's timeline history included. Word count (474) within News range.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, fact-driven tone throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: All five issues from the initial REQUEST_CHANGES review have been addressed. The contributor removed unsupported claims, corrected misattributions, and added three new verified sources (CNBC, Fox Business, TechCrunch Feb 2026). Approved for publication.

content_preview.title: Tesla Debuts Third-Generation Optimus at Shanghai Expo, Targets Mass Production by Year-End
content_preview.summary: Tesla showcased its third-generation Optimus humanoid robot at AWE 2026 in Shanghai, as the company prepares to convert its Fremont factory lines for robot production and aims to begin public sales by end of 2027.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Tesla unveiled its third-generation Optimus humanoid robot at the Appliance & Electronics World Expo (AWE 2026) in Shanghai on March 12, marking the company's first public showing of what it describes as a mass-production-ready humanoid machine. The exhibit signals Tesla's deepening commitment to the Chinese market and its ambition to pivot from automaker to robotics manufacturer.

## What We Know
